frameworks.name: regularised gated neural network
frameworks.one_line: gradient-descent network with gating and noise as model of insight

frameworks.name: insight / aha-moment cognition
frameworks.one_line: human sudden strategy switch traditionally tied to meta-cognition

frameworks.name: gradual associative learning
frameworks.one_line: gradient descent extended to produce abrupt behavioural transitions

core_question: Can simple gradient-descent neural networks reproduce insight-like sudden strategy switches observed in humans?
key_assumptions: input-output associations learned only via gradual gradient descent
regularised gating mechanism in network architecture
stochastic noise injected into gradient updates
manipulations: presence vs absence of hidden task regularity
key_findings: only some humans discover the hidden regularity, with sudden strategy switch
simple regularised networks mimic delay, suddenness, and selectivity of human insight
insight-like switches depend on regularised gating plus gradient noise
networks accumulate 'silent knowledge' initially suppressed by gating
interpretations: insight-like behaviour need not require meta-cognition; it can emerge from gradual learning with gating and noise
regularised gating creates latent representations that surface abruptly as performance jumps
